You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political 
Reform Act.1/
OI'EgTION
1) 
when does a ballot measure qualification co:nmittee which
failed to qualify its neasure for the 1988 baI1ot, and remains
active solety to quatify a Deasure for a future ballot, file its
next campaign disclosure statement?
2l 
can a campaign statenent subrnitted for the qualification
of a ballot measure cover a four year tirne span?
3) 
If the answer to Question 2 is yes, holr.does the
cornmitlee report the rrcumulative anountl of contributions
received?
CONCI.USION
1) 
A comrnittee formed priruarily to qualify a ballot measure
files campaign disclosure statements only after the conclusion of
a successiul or an unsuccessful qualification effort. 
A connittee
which renains active solely for tbe purPose of a future
gualification effort is not required to file semi-annual

2) 
A baltot measure qualification campaign statenent may
cover four years.

3) 
If the campaign staternent covers more than one calendar
year, the cumulative amount of contributions and expenditures on a
qualification statement would include the calendar years covered
by the statement.
rACTA
Arnericans Against Human Suffering is a recipient cornmittee
fonoed to qualify an initiative 
for the state ballot, 
The
cornnittee submitted petitions to qualify their initiative 
for the
Novenber 1988 balIot but failed. 
The connittee filed a
qualification campaign stateDent for the period January t, 1986
through June 6, 1988, in connection with the November 1988
initiative 
drive. 
The coruoittee rernains active solely for the
purpose of qualifying the sane initiative 
for the June 1992
ba llot .
ANAIJYSIS
A comnittee which is formed prinarily to support the
qualification of a ballot measure is required to file its first
campaign disclosure statement (the ttqual if icationl statenent) only
after the culmination of a successful or an unsuccessful
qualification effort. 
(Section 84200. S(tl .,12|
FoIlowing the submission of the qualification statement for
the 1988 initiative 
effort, if the committee remains active solelv
for the puroose of requalifvinq the initiative 
for the 1992
baIIot, the committeers next statement would be the qualification
statenent srhich must be filed 21 days after any peti€ions are
filed or 21 days after ttre deadline for f i).ing petitions,
whichever is earlier. 
The period covered Uy itrL statenrent will
begin on June 7, 1988, and end seven days prior to the filing
deadline. There is no provision in the Act lrhich lirnits the
period covered in a ballot qualification statement. Thus, the
conmittee, s statement rnay cover four years.
Because the qualification activities will extend over nore
than one calendar year, the rrcumulative anountsri of contributions
and expenditures on the qualification statement are calculated
over the entire period covered by the statement.
)l
After filing the qualification statenent the committee,s
future canpaign disclosure obligations will be determined by the
type of committee it then becomes. For example, if the measure
qualifies for the ba1lot in 1992 and the committe" continues
raising and spending funds to support the rneasure, the committee
will becorne a committee prirnarily- formed for the passaqe of a
neasure and will be required to file pre-election carnpaiqn
statements in connection with the eLection. (Section 84r00.5.)


. 
Mary Lolonis
Page 3
Please note the Act does not place linitations 
on the aDount
that a person may contribute to balLot Deasure cornmittees.
(Citizens Acrainst Rent Control v. Berkelev (1981) 454 U.s. 290.)
Therefore, ba11ot Deasure committees and committees formed to
qualify a baLlot measure are not required to report cunulative
contributions on a trfiscal yeartr basis. However, the Commission
may consider changing its advice concerning application of
contribution liroits to such coumittees controll.ed by a candidate
or elected officer. 
(Van Winkle Advice Letter, No. I-89-434, copy
enclosed. )
Please do not hesitate to contact ne at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

sections.question:
1) when does a ballot measure qualification committee which failed to qualify its measure for the 1988 ballot, and remains active solely to qualify a measure for a future ballot, file its next campaign disclosure statement?
2) can a campaign statement submitted for the qualification of a ballot measure cover a four year time span?
3) If the answer to Question 2 is yes, how does the committee report the "cumulative amount" of contributions received?
sections.conclusion:
1) A committee formed primarily to qualify a ballot measure files campaign disclosure statements only after the conclusion of a successful or an unsuccessful qualification effort. A committee which remains active solely for the purpose of a future qualification effort is not required to file semi-annual statements during the qualification effort.
2) A ballot measure qualification campaign statement may cover four years.
3) If the campaign statement covers more than one calendar year, the cumulative amount of contributions and expenditures on a qualification statement would include the calendar years covered by the statement.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks when a ballot measure qualification committee that failed to qualify a measure in 1988 must file its next disclosure statement if it remains active for a future 1992 ballot qualification effort, whether a qualification campaign statement can cover a four-year period, and how cumulative contributions should be reported over multiple years.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A ballot measure qualification committee files disclosure statements only upon conclusion of each qualification effort (successful or unsuccessful); the next filing deadline for the requestor's committee is 21 days after petitions for the 1992 ballot are filed. A qualification statement may cover a four-year period, with cumulative contributions reported across all calendar years included in that statement period.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clearly marked QUESTION, CONCLUSION, FACTS, and ANALYSIS sections. Document contains three related questions with corresponding numbered conclusions. Signed by FPPC General Counsel Kathryn E. Donovan. High confidence in extraction due to clear document structure and complete question/conclusion sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1) when does a ballot measure qualification committee which failed to qualify its measure for the 1988 ballot, and remains active solely to qualify a measure for a future ballot, file its next campaign disclosure statement?
2) can a campaign statement submitted for the qualification of a ballot measure cover a four year time span?
3) If the answer to Question 2 is yes, how does the committee report the "cumulative amount" of contributions received?

CONCLUSION: 1) A committee formed primarily to qualify a ballot measure files campaign disclosure statements only after the conclusion of a successful or an unsuccessful qualification effort. A committee which remains active solely for the purpose of a future qualification effort is not required to file semi-annual statements during the qualification effort.
2) A ballot measure qualification campaign statement may cover four years.
3) If the campaign statement covers more than one calendar year, the cumulative amount of contributions and expenditures on a qualification statement would include the calendar years covered by the statement.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Americans Against Human Suffering on campaign disclosure requirements for a ballot measure qualification committee attempting to qualify an initiative for the 1992 ballot after failing in 1988. The Commission confirms that qualification statements can cover four-year periods and that cumulative contributions should be reported across the entire period covered by the statement.
